title: Error about storage on Ubuntu 22.04
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**
```podman images```  command returned an error about storage:  
```
ERRO[0000] User-selected graph driver "overlay" overwritten by graph driver "vfs" from database - delete libpod local files to resolve
```
file system: btrfs
freshly installed Ubuntu 22.04


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install podman 4.0.3 from source
```
cd ./podman-4.0.3
sudo apt install -y \
  btrfs-progs \
  make \
  git \
  go-md2man \
  iptables \
  golang-1.17 \
  libassuan-dev \
  libbtrfs-dev \
  libc6-dev \
  libdevmapper-dev \
  libglib2.0-dev \
  libgpgme-dev \
  libgpg-error-dev \
  libprotobuf-dev \
  libprotobuf-c-dev \
  libseccomp-dev \
  libselinux1-dev \
  libsystemd-dev \
  pkg-config \
  runc \
  uidmap \
  curl \
  ca-certificates \
  conmon \
  containernetworking-plugins \
  slirp4netns \
  dbus-user-session

sudo ln -fs "/usr/lib/go-1.17/bin/go" "/usr/bin/go"

make clean
sudo make install
sudo make install.completions
sudo mkdir -p /etc/containers
sudo curl --insecure -L -o /etc/containers/registries.conf \
    https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/containers-common/raw/main/f/registries.conf
sudo curl  --insecure -L -o /etc/containers/policy.json \
    https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/containers-common/raw/main/f/default-policy.json

sudo mkdir -p /etc/cni/net.d
curl -qsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/containers/podman/main/cni/87-podman-bridge.conflist \
    | sudo tee /etc/cni/net.d/87-podman-bridge.conflist
```
2.
``` 
podman images
```

**Describe the results you received:**
```
ERRO[0000] User-selected graph driver "overlay" overwritten by graph driver "vfs" from database - delete libpod local files to resolve
REPOSITORY  TAG         IMAGE ID    CREATED     SIZE
```


**Describe the results you expected:**
```
REPOSITORY  TAG         IMAGE ID    CREATED     SIZE
```


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
ERRO[0000] User-selected graph driver "overlay" overwritten by graph driver "vfs" from database - delete libpod local files to resolve
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.3
API Version:  4.0.3
Go Version:   go1.17.3
Built:        Wed Apr 27 15:01:54 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

```
None, build from source
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
VirtualBox

comments.body:
Was `~/.local/share/containers/` empty before you installed it from source?

Does `podman system reset` fix it?

This looks like you ran podman at some point in the past and removed it, but left content in your homedir, now you are running again but it sees previously you used the "vfs" driver and not podman defaults to overlay.

@Luap99 @rhatdan 
It's fresh installed Ubuntu server 22.04 in VBox, podman has not been installed on the system before.
And on fresh  Debian 11 this problem doesn't happen.
```
$ sudo apt autoremove podman buildah
Reading package lists... Done
Building dependency tree... Done
Reading state information... Done
Package 'buildah' is not installed, so not removed
Package 'podman' is not installed, so not removed
0 upgraded, 0 newly installed, 0 to remove and 0 not upgraded.

$ ls ~/.local/share/containers
ls: cannot access '/home/vbn/.local/share/containers': No such file or directory

$ ls ~/.config/containers/
ls: cannot access '/home/vbn/.config/containers/': No such file or directory

```
Reference documentation: https://podman.io/getting-started/installation.html
ls  /home/vbn/.config/containers

> ls /home/vbn/.config/containers

ls: cannot access '/home/vbn/.config/containers/': No such file or directory
@mheon any ideas?
Any chance the containers.conf code logs which config files it reads from? If so, a `podman info --log-level=debug` would tell us.
It merges them together, so no way to tell. I guess it could tell us which it read, perhaps.
The debug logs indicate which configs are merged -> https://github.com/containers/common/blob/main/pkg/config/config.go#L651
Note that this is at trace level, so `--log-level=trace`
Oh, I assumed `debug` includes `trace`. Thanks!

So I am seeing something similar:

`ERRO[0000] User-selected graph driver "vfs" overwritten by graph driver "overlay" from database - delete libpod local files to resolve `

After I run `podman system reset`, I see something else strange:

```
$ podman --log-level debug ps
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug          
DEBU[0000] Called ps.PersistentPreRunE(podman --log-level debug ps) 
DEBU[0000] Merged system config "/usr/share/containers/containers.conf" 
DEBU[0000] Using conmon: "/usr/libexec/podman/conmon"   
DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /home/cam/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db 
DEBU[0000] systemd-logind: The name org.freedesktop.login1 was not provided by any .service files 
DEBU[0000] Using graph driver vfs                       
DEBU[0000] Using graph root /home/cam/.local/share/containers/storage 
DEBU[0000] Using run root /run/user/1000/containers     
DEBU[0000] Using static dir /home/cam/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod 
DEBU[0000] Using tmp dir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp      
DEBU[0000] Using volume path /home/cam/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes 
DEBU[0000] Set libpod namespace to ""                   
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "vfs"   
DEBU[0000] Initializing event backend file              
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime runsc initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runsc: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime krun initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime krun: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime crun initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime crun: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] Configured OCI runtime kata initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime kata: invalid argument 
DEBU[0000] Using OCI runtime "/usr/bin/runc"            
INFO[0000] Setting parallel job count to 37             
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
DEBU[0000] Called ps.PersistentPostRunE(podman --log-level debug ps) 
```

It is still using vfs! This conflicts with what was said above, that podman now defaults to overlay.

```

There's definitely some sort of user-provided config file setting that. Can you try again with `--log-level=trace` so we can figure out which config files were loaded?

Looks like just the normal system-level file.

[podman-trace.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/8654389/podman-trace.txt)

[containers.conf.txt](https://github.com/containers/podman/files/8654394/containers.conf.txt)

The config and the trace look good to me.

Can you share the contents of `/etc/containers/stroage.conf` and `/usr/share/containers/storage.conf`?  Something somewhere must be setting "vfs".
@vrothberg I do not have a either of those files on my system. Neither do I have anything in `~/.config/containers`.

I also started seeing this recently on Gentoo with [podman 4.1.0](https://github.com/gentoo/gentoo/blob/master/app-containers/podman/podman-4.1.0.ebuild). I also don't have any `storage.conf` files in the referenced paths. I do have a `~/.config/containers/short-name-aliases.conf.lock`, but no other files there.

I also use btrfs if that's interesting.
I suggest changing this issue's title to `User-selected graph driver "vfs" overwritten` or similar, since this issue is not unique to Ubuntu.

Well the original reporter had the opposite problem as me, with the "user selected driver" being overlay and it being overwritten as vfs. I can file a separate report if that is desired by the containers team.

Ah I hadn't noticed that difference. I have the same message as you @CameronNemo:

```
ERRO[0000] User-selected graph driver "vfs" overwritten by graph driver "overlay" from database - delete libpod local files to resolve
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Still seeing this issue with podman 4.1?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

I am not seeing the warning, but I imagine that is mostly because I reset my podman. I don't know what other users on my distro have been doing, but nobody has complained yet.

I cannot speak for @xfoobar, especially since his warning was the inverse of mine.
Since you are not seeing it and I have not heard of other complaints, I am closing.  Reopen if this happens again.

Since Google leads to this issue when you search for the error message, I wanted to add (and hopefully save others some time):

1. This happens when you install `podman` from the Ubuntu 22.04 repository without also installing `fuse-overlayfs` (which is only a Recommended or "soft" dependency).
2. The fix that worked for me was to first install `fuse-overlayfs` and then afterward run `rm -rf ~/.local/share/containers/` (as mentioned in a comment on #5114).

Following @jlindgren90 's example, google led me to this issue. I had this problem when I upgraded from Ubuntu 22.10 to 23.04 . I already had `fuse-overlayfs` installed. Running `sudo rm -rf ~/.local/share/containers` got rid of the error message.
